steps.title: Subtract to target 4x directly
steps.content: The question asks for $4x$, so avoid solving the whole system. Subtract the second equation from the first: $$2x=9+7y$$ $$-2x=3+7y.$$

steps.title: Cancel y and read the answer
steps.content: The subtraction gives $$2x-(-2x)=(9+7y)-(3+7y).$$ The $7y$ terms cancel, leaving $$4x=6.$$ Since this is exactly what the question asks for, the answer is 6.

steps.title: Check with the intersection
steps.content: The two lines intersect at $x=1.5$. Substituting that value into the requested expression gives $$4x=4(1.5)=6,$$ which agrees with the elimination shortcut.
